content.problemStatement:
Chef has three water bottles. At any point, if at least two of them are empty, she will fill them up. But if at most one bottle is empty, she will wait, and not fill them up now.

You are given three integers - B1,B2,B_1, B_2,B1​,B2​, and B3B_3B3​. 

If B1=1B_1 = 1B1​=1, it means that the first bottle is full. 

If B1=0B_1 = 0B1​=0, it means that the first bottle is empty. 

Similarly, B2B_2B2​ denotes whether the second bottle is full or empty, and B3B_3B3​ denotes it for the third bottle.

Output "Water filling time", if Chef has to fill the bottles now. If not, output "Not now".


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers, B1,B2,B3B_1, B_2, B_3B1​,B2​,B3​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, either "Water filling time", or "Not now".
constraints: Bi is either 0 or 1 for i = 1,2,3
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers B1 B2 B3, where each Bi is either 0 (empty) or 1 (full).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing either "Water filling time" if at least two bottles are empty, or "Not now" otherwise.

sampleTestCases.output:
Water filling time
Not now
Not now
Water filling time
Not now
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Testcase 1: all three are empty → at least two empty → Water filling time.
Testcase 2: all three are full → no empty bottle → Not now.
Testcase 3: only one empty (B3) → Not now.
Testcase 4: two empty (B1 and B3) → Water filling time.
Testcase 5: only one empty (B1) → Not now.
Two bottles (B1 and B3) are empty, so Chef must fill them now.
Only one bottle (B2) is empty, therefore Chef waits.

solution.approach: For each test case count how many of the three values are 0. If the count is greater than or equal to 2, print "Water filling time"; otherwise print "Not now". This requires only a few integer operations per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
